142.reason: [This condition is incorrect: after the while loop, p already points to the closing quote or end of line; checking p+1 is out of bounds and logic is flawed]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect: after the while loop, 'p' points to the first '\'' or beyond. The check 'p + 1 < chars.length && chars[p] == '\''' is flawed because if the while loop exited due to finding '\'', then chars[p] is already '\'', so checking p+1 is unnecessary and wrong. Also, if the closing quote is missing, no error is reported. But more importantly, this block does not handle unterminated character constants, which could be another error type—but since only error 'a' is expected, the primary bug is elsewhere.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect: after the while loop, p points to the closing quote or beyond; checking p+1 here is wrong and may skip error detection for unterminated char literals]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition is wrong: after the while loop, p points to the closing quote (if found), so checking p+1 is incorrect. Also, if the closing quote is at the end of the line (p == chars.length - 1), then p+1 >= length, and this block is skipped, leading to missing CHRCON output. Moreover, if there's no closing quote, the error isn't handled.]

Moreover, it fails to output "DIV /" in cases like "/x", leading to missing token output. However, the critical error related to test failure is the lack of early termination after setting errSym = false.]
